intent: Check whether the candidate understands AWS shared responsibility and can turn it into concrete engineering decisions for a deployed workload.
primaryQuestion: For a web application running on EC2 with RDS and S3, what is AWS responsible for and what is your team still responsible for?

expectedSignals.label: Separates security of the cloud from security in the cloud.

requirements.description: Explains AWS responsibility for security of the cloud infrastructure
Explains customer responsibility for security in their cloud workload
Applies the distinction to an EC2, RDS, and S3 web application

expectedSignals.label: Names customer-owned tasks such as IAM permissions, network exposure, patching choices, encryption configuration, backups, logging, and application vulnerabilities.

requirements.description: Names IAM permissions and network exposure as customer-owned controls
Mentions patching choices, encryption configuration, or backups
Mentions logging and application vulnerability management

expectedSignals.label: Explains that responsibility changes by service model, for example EC2 versus managed database or object storage.

requirements.description: Explains that responsibility changes by AWS service model
Contrasts EC2 as more customer-managed than managed services
Uses RDS or S3 as examples where AWS handles more of the underlying infrastructure

expectedSignals.label: Connects the answer to Well-Architected pillars such as security, reliability, operational excellence, and cost optimization.

requirements.description: Connects shared responsibility to the security pillar
Mentions reliability or operational excellence implications
Mentions cost optimization where backup, logging, retention, or managed service choices affect cost

scoringHints.description: Gives service-specific responsibilities and explains how the team would verify controls in production.
Understands shared responsibility but stays mostly at a conceptual level.
Knows AWS manages physical infrastructure but misses most application and configuration responsibilities.
Answer is vague or treats cloud as fully managed by default.

followUps.question: How would your answer change if the compute layer moved from EC2 to Lambda?
Name three controls your team would configure before going live.
followUps.purpose: Force concrete operational and security controls.

en.title: AWS shared responsibility in practice
en.displayQuestion: For a web application running on EC2 with RDS and S3, what is AWS responsible for and what is your team still responsible for?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can apply shared responsibility to real AWS services.
guidance: Ask for service-specific examples.
Listen for security, reliability, operations, and cost ownership.
commonMistakes: Saying AWS owns all security.
Giving a definition without deployment controls.

vi.title: Shared responsibility trên AWS
vi.displayQuestion: Với một web application chạy trên EC2, RDS và S3, AWS chịu trách nhiệm phần nào và team của bạn vẫn phải chịu trách nhiệm phần nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng áp dụng shared responsibility vào các dịch vụ AWS thực tế.
guidance: Yêu cầu ví dụ cụ thể theo từng dịch vụ.
Nghe xem ứng viên có nhắc tới bảo mật, độ tin cậy, vận hành và chi phí không.
commonMistakes: Nói AWS chịu toàn bộ bảo mật.
Chỉ nêu định nghĩa mà không nói control khi triển khai.

ja.title: AWSの責任共有モデル
ja.displayQuestion: EC2、RDS、S3で動くWebアプリでは、AWSが責任を持つ範囲とチームが責任を持つ範囲は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 責任共有モデルを実際のAWSサービスに適用できるか確認する。
guidance: サービスごとの具体例を求める。
セキュリティ、信頼性、運用、コストの責任を聞き取る。
commonMistakes: AWSがすべてのセキュリティを担当すると考える。
定義だけで実運用の管理策を説明しない。

sourceReferences.label: AWS Well-Architected Framework: The pillars
sourceReferences.note: Used for pillar framing and architecture review expectations.

sourceReferences.label: AWS Well-Architected Tool: Framework overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for workload review and trade-off framing.

intent: Assess practical understanding of IAM identities, roles, temporary credentials, least privilege, and policy scope.
primaryQuestion: How would you give an application running on EC2 permission to read only one S3 bucket without using long-lived access keys?

expectedSignals.label: Uses an IAM role attached through an EC2 instance profile instead of static credentials on disk.

requirements.description: Uses an EC2 instance profile to attach an IAM role to the instance
Avoids long-lived static credentials on disk, in .env files, or CI variables
Explains that the application receives permissions through the attached role

expectedSignals.label: Scopes the policy to the specific bucket and needed actions such as s3:GetObject and possibly s3:ListBucket.

requirements.description: Scopes the policy resource to the specific S3 bucket or object prefix
Includes the needed read action such as s3:GetObject
Includes s3:ListBucket only when listing bucket keys is required

expectedSignals.label: Mentions temporary credentials automatically provided to the workload.

requirements.description: Mentions temporary credentials rather than permanent access keys
Explains that credentials are provided and rotated automatically for the workload
Mentions the SDK or workload can retrieve role credentials without hard-coded secrets

expectedSignals.label: Considers policy conditions, access review, and avoiding broad managed policies in production.

requirements.description: Considers IAM policy conditions such as source VPC endpoint, prefix, encryption, or tags
Mentions reviewing access over time
Avoids broad policies such as full S3 access in production

scoringHints.description: Designs a least-privilege role-based setup and names concrete actions, resources, and verification steps.
Chooses IAM roles and avoids access keys but misses finer policy scoping details.
Understands permissions are needed but reaches for broad or long-lived credentials.
Answer does not show usable IAM knowledge.

followUps.question: When would you use an IAM policy condition in this setup?
What risk does an access key in a CI variable or .env file create?

en.title: Least-privilege IAM access for a workload
en.displayQuestion: How would you give an application running on EC2 permission to read only one S3 bucket without using long-lived access keys?
en.displayIntent: Check IAM roles, temporary credentials, and least-privilege policy design.
guidance: Ask for specific IAM policy scope.
Look for role-based workload identity instead of static secrets.
commonMistakes: Using an IAM user access key for an EC2 workload.
Granting wildcard permissions because it is faster.

vi.title: IAM least privilege cho workload
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn cấp quyền cho application chạy trên EC2 đọc duy nhất một S3 bucket như thế nào mà không dùng access key dài hạn?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra IAM role, temporary credential và thiết kế policy theo least privilege.
guidance: Hỏi rõ scope của IAM policy.
Ưu tiên workload identity bằng role thay vì secret tĩnh.
commonMistakes: Dùng access key của IAM user cho workload EC2.
Cấp wildcard permission vì nhanh hơn.

ja.title: ワークロード向け最小権限IAM
ja.displayQuestion: 長期アクセスキーを使わずに、EC2上のアプリへ特定のS3バケットだけを読み取る権限をどう付与しますか。
ja.displayIntent: IAMロール、一時認証情報、最小権限ポリシー設計を確認する。
guidance: IAMポリシーの具体的な範囲を聞く。
静的シークレットではなくロールベースのワークロードIDを重視する。
commonMistakes: EC2ワークロードにIAMユーザーのアクセスキーを使う。
速さを理由にワイルドカード権限を付ける。

sourceReferences.label: AWS IAM security best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for temporary credentials, least privilege, MFA, and credential hygiene.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can reason about VPC routing, public and private subnets, internet gateways, and NAT gateways.
primaryQuestion: Design a VPC with public web entry points and private application instances that need outbound internet access for updates. What routes and gateways are required?

expectedSignals.label: Places public-facing load balancer or bastion-like entry points in public subnets with routes to an internet gateway.

requirements.description: Places the public-facing ALB or entry point in public subnets
Includes a route to an internet gateway for public subnets
Explains that the route table and IGW route make the subnet public

expectedSignals.label: Places application instances in private subnets without a direct route to an internet gateway.

requirements.description: Places application instances in private subnets
States private subnets do not have a direct route to the internet gateway
Explains inbound traffic reaches private instances through the public entry point or load balancer

expectedSignals.label: Uses a NAT gateway in a public subnet for private subnet outbound IPv4 internet access.

requirements.description: Places a NAT gateway in a public subnet
Routes private subnet outbound IPv4 internet traffic to the NAT gateway
Discusses high availability trade-offs such as NAT gateways per Availability Zone

expectedSignals.label: Explains route table association per subnet and separates routing from security group rules.

requirements.description: Explains route table association per subnet
Separates routing behavior from security group rules
Mentions public and private subnets can use different route tables

scoringHints.description: Draws a correct route model across multiple AZs and explains why each route exists.
Understands public/private subnet routing but misses HA or route-table association details.
Knows the terms but cannot describe the packet path.
Answer is mostly service names without routing logic.

followUps.question: What exactly makes a subnet public in AWS?
What changes if you want high availability across two Availability Zones?
followUps.purpose: Check NAT gateway and subnet design trade-offs.

en.title: VPC public and private subnet routing
en.displayQuestion: Design a VPC with public web entry points and private application instances that need outbound internet access for updates. What routes and gateways are required?
en.displayIntent: Check VPC routing, subnet classification, internet gateway, and NAT gateway reasoning.
guidance: Ask the candidate to describe the packet path.
Separate routing decisions from firewall decisions.
commonMistakes: Thinking subnet name alone controls internet access.
Using one NAT design without discussing AZ resilience.

vi.title: Route public/private subnet trong VPC
vi.displayQuestion: Hãy thiết kế một VPC có điểm vào public cho web và các application instance private cần outbound internet để update. Cần những route và gateway nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra tư duy về VPC routing, phân loại subnet, internet gateway và NAT gateway.
guidance: Yêu cầu ứng viên mô tả đường đi của packet.
Tách rõ quyết định routing và quyết định firewall.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ tên subnet tự quyết định internet access.
Dùng một NAT design mà không bàn về HA theo AZ.

ja.title: VPCのpublic/private subnetルーティング
ja.displayQuestion: 公開Web入口と、更新のために外向きインターネット接続が必要なprivateアプリケーションインスタンスを持つVPCを設計してください。必要なルートとゲートウェイは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: VPCルーティング、サブネット分類、Internet Gateway、NAT Gatewayの理解を確認する。
guidance: パケットの経路を説明してもらう。
ルーティングとファイアウォールの判断を分けて聞く。
commonMistakes: サブネット名だけでインターネット接続が決まると考える。
AZ冗長性を考えずにNATを1つだけ置く。

sourceReferences.label: Amazon VPC route tables
sourceReferences.note: Used for route table and route target concepts.

sourceReferences.label: Amazon VPC internet gateway
sourceReferences.note: Used for public subnet and internet gateway routing behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Amazon VPC NAT gateways
sourceReferences.note: Used for private subnet outbound connectivity.

intent: Assess troubleshooting depth for AWS network reachability issues involving security groups, network ACLs, routes, and target health.
primaryQuestion: An EC2 instance in a private subnet cannot reach an external package registry, and the ALB health check is failing. How would you debug this?

expectedSignals.label: Checks route tables, NAT gateway presence, NAT subnet route to internet gateway, and subnet associations.

requirements.description: Checks route tables for private and public subnet routes
Checks NAT gateway presence and NAT subnet route to the internet gateway
Checks that affected subnets are associated with the expected route tables

expectedSignals.label: Verifies security group egress and inbound rules from the ALB security group to the instance port.

requirements.description: Verifies instance or workload security group egress
Verifies inbound rules from the ALB security group to the instance port
Explains that stateful security groups can use narrower source references than broad NACL rules

expectedSignals.label: Checks network ACL stateless rules for inbound and outbound ephemeral ports.

requirements.description: Explains network ACLs are stateless
Checks both inbound and outbound NACL rules
Mentions ephemeral port rules for return traffic

expectedSignals.label: Uses ALB target health reason codes, instance logs, and connectivity tests instead of guessing.

requirements.description: Uses ALB target health reason codes
Checks instance or application logs
Runs concrete connectivity tests instead of guessing

scoringHints.description: Builds a step-by-step reachability investigation and explains stateful versus stateless filtering.
Checks the main AWS network components but misses a detail such as ephemeral ports or ALB reason codes.
Mentions common components but does not order the diagnosis by traffic path.
Only suggests broad firewall changes or restarts.

followUps.question: Walk through the return path for an outbound HTTPS request through a NAT gateway.
Why can a security group rule be narrower than a network ACL rule in many designs?
followUps.purpose: Check stateful versus stateless filtering awareness.

en.title: Debug AWS network reachability
en.displayQuestion: An EC2 instance in a private subnet cannot reach an external package registry, and the ALB health check is failing. How would you debug this?
en.displayIntent: Check practical troubleshooting across routes, NAT, security groups, NACLs, and ALB target health.
guidance: Ask for ordered checks by traffic path.
Watch for evidence-driven debugging rather than broad rule changes.
commonMistakes: Opening every port to the internet.
Treating outbound internet access and ALB health checks as the same flow.

vi.title: Debug reachability mạng AWS
vi.displayQuestion: Một EC2 instance trong private subnet không gọi được package registry bên ngoài, đồng thời ALB health check fail. Bạn debug như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng debug thực tế qua route, NAT, security group, NACL và ALB target health.
guidance: Yêu cầu thứ tự kiểm tra theo traffic path.
Quan sát xem ứng viên debug bằng chứng cứ hay chỉ mở rule rộng.
commonMistakes: Mở toàn bộ port ra internet.
Gộp outbound internet access và ALB health check thành cùng một luồng.

ja.title: AWSネットワーク到達性のデバッグ
ja.displayQuestion: private subnetのEC2が外部パッケージレジストリへ接続できず、ALBのhealth checkも失敗しています。どうデバッグしますか。
ja.displayIntent: ルート、NAT、Security Group、NACL、ALB target healthをまたぐ実践的な調査力を確認する。
guidance: 通信経路に沿った確認順序を求める。
広いルール変更ではなく証拠に基づく調査かを見る。
commonMistakes: すべてのポートをインターネットへ開ける。
外向き通信とALB health checkを同じフローとして扱う。

sourceReferences.label: Amazon VPC route table concepts
sourceReferences.note: Used for route destination and target concepts.

sourceReferences.label: Application Load Balancer target health checks
sourceReferences.note: Used for ALB health check behavior and target status.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can design and operate an EC2 Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer.
primaryQuestion: How would you run a stateless API on EC2 so it survives instance failure and scales with traffic?

expectedSignals.label: Uses an Auto Scaling group across multiple subnets and Availability Zones behind an Application Load Balancer.

requirements.description: Uses an Auto Scaling group across multiple Availability Zones
Mentions multiple subnets for instance placement
Places the API behind an Application Load Balancer

expectedSignals.label: Defines launch template content such as AMI, instance type, security groups, user data, and instance profile.

requirements.description: Mentions AMI and instance type in the launch template
Mentions security groups and user data
Mentions an instance profile or IAM role for the instances

expectedSignals.label: Configures health checks, grace period, desired/min/max capacity, and scaling policy based on useful metrics.

requirements.description: Configures health checks and grace period
Configures desired, minimum, and maximum capacity
Chooses a scaling policy based on useful metrics such as request count, latency, CPU, or queue depth

expectedSignals.label: Keeps application state outside the instance and plans rolling or immutable deployments.

requirements.description: Keeps application state outside EC2 instances
Mentions using external storage such as RDS, S3, cache, or session store for state
Plans rolling or immutable deployments

scoringHints.description: Explains a full ASG and ALB design including health, scaling signals, statelessness, and deployment behavior.
Covers ASG and ALB basics but misses metrics or deployment details.
Knows Auto Scaling exists but cannot describe how replacement and health checks work.
Answer is a list of AWS service names without operational design.

followUps.question: Which metric would you scale on and why?
followUps.purpose: Check whether scaling is tied to workload behavior.

followUps.question: What should happen if an instance passes EC2 status checks but the app returns 500 on /health?
followUps.purpose: Probe load balancer and application health semantics.

en.title: EC2 Auto Scaling for a stateless API
en.displayQuestion: How would you run a stateless API on EC2 so it survives instance failure and scales with traffic?
en.displayIntent: Check Auto Scaling, ALB, health checks, scaling policy, and stateless service design.
guidance: Ask for capacity bounds and health check behavior.
Look for state externalization and deployment strategy.
commonMistakes: Treating one EC2 instance as resilient.
Scaling on a metric that does not represent user demand.

vi.title: EC2 Auto Scaling cho stateless API
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn chạy một stateless API trên EC2 như thế nào để chịu được instance failure và scale theo traffic?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra Auto Scaling, ALB, health check, scaling policy và thiết kế stateless service.
guidance: Hỏi rõ min/max/desired capacity và health check.
Tìm dấu hiệu ứng viên biết đưa state ra ngoài instance và có chiến lược deploy.
commonMistakes: Xem một EC2 instance là đủ resilient.
Scale theo metric không phản ánh demand của user.

ja.title: stateless API向けEC2 Auto Scaling
ja.displayQuestion: EC2上のstateless APIを、インスタンス障害に耐えつつトラフィックに応じてスケールさせるにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: Auto Scaling、ALB、health check、scaling policy、stateless設計を確認する。
guidance: 容量範囲とhealth checkの動作を聞く。
状態の外部化とデプロイ戦略に注目する。
commonMistakes: EC2 1台だけで耐障害性があると考える。
ユーザー需要を表さないメトリクスでスケールする。

sourceReferences.label: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling launch templates
sourceReferences.note: Used for launch template and instance configuration concepts.

sourceReferences.label: Auto Scaling health checks
sourceReferences.note: Used for replacement behavior and health check sources.

sourceReferences.label: Application Load Balancer target groups
sourceReferences.note: Used for target groups and per-target health checks.

intent: Check whether the candidate can design safe S3 storage with access control, versioning, lifecycle, and recovery behavior.
primaryQuestion: How would you store user-uploaded documents in S3 so they are private, recoverable after accidental deletion, and cost-aware over time?

expectedSignals.label: Keeps the bucket private and uses S3 Block Public Access unless public access is intentionally required.

requirements.description: Keeps the S3 bucket private by default
Uses S3 Block Public Access to guard against accidental public policy or ACL exposure
States public access should be explicitly justified if ever required

expectedSignals.label: Uses IAM or application-mediated access instead of public object ACLs.

requirements.description: Uses IAM policies or roles to control access
Mentions application-mediated access such as authenticated download or presigned URLs
Avoids public object ACLs for private user documents

expectedSignals.label: Enables versioning to recover overwritten or deleted objects and understands delete markers.

requirements.description: Enables S3 versioning for the bucket
Explains recovery from overwritten or deleted objects
Mentions delete markers in versioned buckets

expectedSignals.label: Uses lifecycle rules to transition or expire old versions and archival data based on access patterns.

requirements.description: Uses lifecycle rules for old object versions
Mentions transitioning data to cheaper storage classes or archival tiers
Expires or transitions data based on access patterns and retention needs

scoringHints.description: Balances privacy, recovery, lifecycle cost, and application access patterns with concrete S3 settings.
Covers private bucket and versioning but misses lifecycle cost or delete marker behavior.
Knows S3 stores files but has weak access control or recovery design.
Answer relies on public buckets or manual backup only.

followUps.question: What cost issue can versioning introduce, and how would lifecycle rules control it?
If a public bucket policy is added accidentally, what setting can still block public access?

en.title: Private and recoverable S3 object storage
en.displayQuestion: How would you store user-uploaded documents in S3 so they are private, recoverable after accidental deletion, and cost-aware over time?
en.displayIntent: Check S3 privacy, versioning, lifecycle, and access-control design.
guidance: Ask for exact S3 features.
Look for cost and recovery trade-offs, not only storage.
commonMistakes: Using a public bucket for app convenience.
Enabling versioning without lifecycle management.

vi.title: S3 private và recoverable
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn lưu document người dùng upload lên S3 như thế nào để object private, có thể khôi phục khi xóa nhầm và vẫn tối ưu chi phí theo thời gian?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra thiết kế S3 về privacy, versioning, lifecycle và access control.
guidance: Hỏi rõ feature S3 cụ thể.
Tìm trade-off giữa chi phí và khả năng phục hồi, không chỉ lưu trữ.
commonMistakes: Dùng public bucket cho tiện.
Bật versioning nhưng không quản lý lifecycle.

ja.title: privateで復旧可能なS3保存
ja.displayQuestion: ユーザーがアップロードした文書を、privateで、誤削除から復旧でき、長期的なコストも考慮してS3に保存するにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: S3の公開制御、versioning、lifecycle、アクセス制御設計を確認する。
guidance: 具体的なS3機能を聞く。
保存だけでなく復旧とコストのトレードオフを見る。
commonMistakes: アプリの都合でpublic bucketを使う。
versioningを有効にしてlifecycleを管理しない。

sourceReferences.label: S3 Block Public Access
sourceReferences.note: Used for public access controls.

sourceReferences.label: S3 Versioning
sourceReferences.note: Used for recovery from accidental delete or overwrite.

sourceReferences.label: S3 Lifecycle configuration
sourceReferences.note: Used for transition and expiration rules.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands Lambda invocation modes, retry behavior, duplicate events, and idempotency.
primaryQuestion: A Lambda function processes payment events asynchronously. How would you handle retries, duplicate delivery, and failed events safely?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that asynchronous Lambda invocation retries function errors and can deliver the same event more than once.

requirements.description: Explains asynchronous Lambda invocation retries function errors
Mentions the same event can be delivered more than once
Connects duplicate delivery to duplicate payment side-effect risk

expectedSignals.label: Designs idempotency using a stable event or payment id and conditional writes or deduplication records.

requirements.description: Uses a stable event id or payment id for idempotency
Mentions conditional writes or compare-and-set behavior
Mentions deduplication records or idempotency keys

expectedSignals.label: Configures an on-failure destination or dead-letter queue for events that exhaust processing attempts.

requirements.description: Configures an on-failure destination
Mentions a dead-letter queue for failed asynchronous events
Explains this path is for events that exhaust processing attempts

expectedSignals.label: Monitors failures, throttles, event age, and destination delivery failures.

requirements.description: Monitors Lambda failures or error rate
Monitors throttles and event age
Monitors destination or DLQ delivery failures

scoringHints.description: Combines Lambda retry semantics with idempotent business logic, failure capture, and monitoring.
Understands retries and DLQ but gives limited idempotency implementation detail.
Mentions retrying but does not control duplicate side effects.
Answer treats Lambda as exactly-once processing.

followUps.question: What happens if the same payment event is delivered twice after the first invocation succeeded?
Which CloudWatch metrics or alarms would you add for this path?

en.title: Lambda retries and idempotency
en.displayQuestion: A Lambda function processes payment events asynchronously. How would you handle retries, duplicate delivery, and failed events safely?
en.displayIntent: Check Lambda retry behavior, idempotency, DLQ/destinations, and monitoring.
guidance: Push on duplicate delivery.
Ask how failed events are replayed or inspected.
commonMistakes: Assuming exactly-once execution.
Retrying external side effects without idempotency.

vi.title: Lambda retry và idempotency
vi.displayQuestion: Một Lambda xử lý payment event theo async invocation. Bạn xử lý retry, duplicate delivery và failed event an toàn như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra retry behavior của Lambda, idempotency, DLQ/destination và monitoring.
guidance: Đào sâu tình huống event bị giao trùng.
Hỏi cách inspect hoặc replay failed event.
commonMistakes: Giả định Lambda chạy exactly once.
Retry side effect bên ngoài mà không có idempotency.

ja.title: Lambdaのretryとidempotency
ja.displayQuestion: Lambdaがpayment eventを非同期で処理します。retry、重複配信、failed eventを安全に扱うにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: Lambdaのretry、idempotency、DLQ/destination、監視を確認する。
guidance: 重複配信について深掘りする。
failed eventの調査や再処理方法を聞く。
commonMistakes: Lambdaがexactly onceで実行されると仮定する。
idempotencyなしで外部副作用をretryする。

sourceReferences.label: Lambda retry behavior
sourceReferences.note: Used for invocation-mode retry behavior and duplicate handling.

sourceReferences.label: Lambda asynchronous error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for async retries, throttles, event age, and duplicate events.

sourceReferences.label: Lambda async destinations and DLQs
sourceReferences.note: Used for capturing failed invocation records.

intent: Evaluate compute platform selection across Lambda, ECS, and EC2 based on workload shape and operational constraints.
primaryQuestion: For a new image-processing service with bursty traffic and some jobs taking several minutes, how would you choose between Lambda, ECS/Fargate, and EC2?

expectedSignals.label: Compares operational burden, startup latency, execution duration, concurrency, package size, networking, and cost profile.

requirements.description: Compares operational burden and startup latency
Compares execution duration, concurrency, and package/dependency size
Compares networking needs and cost profile

expectedSignals.label: Uses Lambda for event-driven bursty tasks when duration and runtime limits fit.

requirements.description: Uses Lambda for event-driven bursty tasks
Checks that duration and runtime limits fit
Considers Lambda concurrency and cost behavior for bursts

expectedSignals.label: Uses ECS/Fargate for containerized long-running workers or larger dependencies with less server management.

requirements.description: Uses ECS/Fargate for containerized workers
Mentions long-running jobs or larger dependencies as ECS/Fargate fits
Mentions reduced server management compared with EC2

expectedSignals.label: Uses EC2 when deep host control, specialized capacity, or steady high utilization justifies the operational cost.

requirements.description: Uses EC2 when deep host control is required
Mentions specialized capacity such as GPU or custom host requirements
Mentions steady high utilization justifying EC2 operational cost

scoringHints.description: Frames the choice around measurable workload constraints and proposes a queue-backed, observable processing design.
Compares the services correctly but lacks concrete sizing or failure handling.
Knows high-level differences but cannot map them to this workload.
Answer is preference-based rather than technical.

followUps.question: What measurements would you collect before finalizing the compute choice?
How would the decision change if jobs regularly run longer than the Lambda limit or need GPU acceleration?

en.title: Choosing Lambda, ECS/Fargate, or EC2
en.displayQuestion: For a new image-processing service with bursty traffic and some jobs taking several minutes, how would you choose between Lambda, ECS/Fargate, and EC2?
en.displayIntent: Check compute trade-off reasoning for workload shape, operations, and cost.
guidance: Ask for constraints and metrics before the choice.
Look for queueing, backpressure, and failure handling.
commonMistakes: Picking the favorite service by habit.
Ignoring duration, dependency size, or concurrency limits.

vi.title: Chọn Lambda, ECS/Fargate hay EC2
vi.displayQuestion: Với một service xử lý ảnh mới, traffic bursty và có job chạy vài phút, bạn chọn Lambda, ECS/Fargate hay EC2 như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra tư duy trade-off compute theo workload shape, vận hành và chi phí.
guidance: Hỏi constraint và metric trước khi chọn.
Tìm dấu hiệu có queueing, backpressure và xử lý failure.
commonMistakes: Chọn service quen dùng theo cảm tính.
Bỏ qua giới hạn thời gian chạy, dependency size hoặc concurrency.

ja.title: Lambda、ECS/Fargate、EC2の選択

guidance: 選択前に制約とメトリクスを聞く。
queueing、backpressure、failure handlingに注目する。
commonMistakes: 慣れたサービスを好みで選ぶ。
実行時間、依存関係サイズ、concurrency制限を無視する。

intent: Check understanding of RDS availability, read scaling, failover, backups, and operational limits.
primaryQuestion: How do Multi-AZ deployments and read replicas solve different problems in Amazon RDS?

expectedSignals.label: Explains Multi-AZ as an availability and failover feature for the primary database.

requirements.description: Explains Multi-AZ as an availability feature
Mentions failover for the primary database
Clarifies Multi-AZ is not mainly for read scaling in classic deployments

expectedSignals.label: Explains read replicas as read scaling or reporting offload, not automatic write failover by default.

requirements.description: Explains read replicas as read scaling
Mentions reporting or analytical read offload
Clarifies read replicas are not automatic write failover by default

expectedSignals.label: Mentions backups, snapshots, recovery objectives, replication lag, and application connection behavior during failover.

requirements.description: Mentions backups, snapshots, or recovery objectives
Mentions replication lag and its user-visible consistency impact
Mentions application connection behavior during failover

expectedSignals.label: Understands that the Multi-AZ standby is not used for serving normal read traffic in classic deployments.

requirements.description: States the classic Multi-AZ standby is not a normal read target
Explains the standby exists for failover
Explains a read replica is needed for normal read offload

scoringHints.description: Clearly separates HA, read scaling, backup, failover, and lag with real application implications.
Knows the main difference but misses operational details such as lag or connection handling.
Confuses replicas, backups, and standby instances.
Answer is a generic database availability statement.

followUps.question: What user-visible issue can replication lag create if reads go to replicas?
Which RDS signals would you monitor for failover readiness and replica health?

en.title: RDS Multi-AZ versus read replicas
en.displayQuestion: How do Multi-AZ deployments and read replicas solve different problems in Amazon RDS?
en.displayIntent: Check RDS HA, read scaling, backups, failover, and replication-lag reasoning.
guidance: Ask for an example read/write workload.
Listen for failover and application connection behavior.
commonMistakes: Using Multi-AZ standby for normal reads.
Treating replicas as a complete backup strategy.

vi.title: RDS Multi-AZ và read replica
vi.displayQuestion: Multi-AZ deployment và read replica trong Amazon RDS giải quyết những vấn đề khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra HA, read scaling, backup, failover và replication lag trong RDS.
guidance: Yêu cầu ví dụ workload đọc/ghi.
Nghe xem ứng viên có nói tới failover và reconnect của application không.
commonMistakes: Dùng Multi-AZ standby cho read traffic thông thường.
Xem replica là chiến lược backup đầy đủ.

ja.title: RDS Multi-AZとread replica
ja.displayQuestion: Amazon RDSのMulti-AZ deploymentとread replicaは、それぞれどの問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: RDSのHA、read scaling、backup、failover、replication lagを確認する。
guidance: 読み書きワークロードの例を求める。
failover時のアプリ接続動作に触れるかを見る。
commonMistakes: 通常読み取りにMulti-AZ standbyを使うと考える。
replicaを完全なbackup戦略として扱う。

intent: Assess query-driven DynamoDB modeling, partition key design, secondary indexes, and hot partition awareness.
primaryQuestion: How would you model an orders table in DynamoDB when you need to query by customer, by order id, and by order status dashboard?

expectedSignals.label: Starts from access patterns instead of normalizing first.

requirements.description: Starts DynamoDB modeling from required access patterns
Identifies queries by customer, order id, and status dashboard
Avoids starting from relational normalization as the primary design method

expectedSignals.label: Chooses partition and sort keys that distribute load and support primary lookups.

requirements.description: Chooses partition keys that distribute load
Uses sort keys to support ordered or grouped lookups
Supports primary lookups such as by customer or order id

expectedSignals.label: Uses a global secondary index when querying by status or another access pattern not served by the base key.

requirements.description: Uses a GSI for order status dashboard queries when not served by the base key
Explains GSIs support access patterns outside the base table key
Chooses projected attributes needed by the dashboard query

expectedSignals.label: Discusses hot partitions, high-cardinality keys, projected attributes, consistency, and write amplification.

requirements.description: Discusses hot partition risk such as low-cardinality orderStatus keys
Discusses projected attributes and consistency expectations
Mentions write amplification from maintaining indexes

scoringHints.description: Derives keys and GSIs from access patterns and explains load distribution, consistency, and cost trade-offs.
Understands keys and GSIs but misses hot-key or projection implications.
Can define partition keys but does not reason from queries.
Answer is relational modeling transplanted into DynamoDB.

followUps.question: Why can a GSI on orderStatus become a problem on a high-volume system?
followUps.purpose: Check hot partition and low-cardinality key awareness.

followUps.question: Which attributes would you project into the dashboard index and why?
followUps.purpose: Probe index size and query efficiency.

en.title: DynamoDB access-pattern modeling
en.displayQuestion: How would you model an orders table in DynamoDB when you need to query by customer, by order id, and by order status dashboard?
en.displayIntent: Check DynamoDB key design, GSIs, hot partitions, and query-driven modeling.
guidance: Start with access patterns.
Ask about write cost and eventual consistency of indexes.
commonMistakes: Modeling for joins first.
Using low-cardinality partition keys without a load strategy.

vi.title: Model DynamoDB theo access pattern
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn model bảng orders trong DynamoDB như thế nào khi cần query theo customer, theo order id và dashboard theo order status?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra key design, GSI, hot partition và modeling theo query trong DynamoDB.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ access pattern.
Hỏi về write cost và eventual consistency của index.
commonMistakes: Model theo join trước.
Dùng partition key ít giá trị mà không có chiến lược phân tải.

ja.title: DynamoDBのaccess pattern設計

guidance: access patternから始める。
indexのwrite costとeventual consistencyを聞く。

intent: Check whether the candidate understands SQS visibility timeout, deletion, retries, DLQs, and idempotent consumers.
primaryQuestion: How would you build an SQS consumer that processes jobs reliably without losing messages or doing unsafe duplicate work?

expectedSignals.label: Explains receive, visibility timeout, successful processing, and DeleteMessage.

requirements.description: Explains receiving messages from SQS
Explains visibility timeout during processing
Explains DeleteMessage after successful processing

expectedSignals.label: Sets visibility timeout based on processing time and extends it for long jobs when needed.

requirements.description: Sets visibility timeout based on expected processing time
Mentions extending visibility timeout for long-running jobs
Explains that a message can be delivered again if processing exceeds visibility timeout

expectedSignals.label: Uses DLQ with maxReceiveCount for poison messages and diagnostic replay.

requirements.description: Uses a dead-letter queue for poison messages
Configures maxReceiveCount
Mentions diagnostics and controlled replay of failed messages

expectedSignals.label: Designs idempotent processing because standard queues can deliver messages more than once.

requirements.description: Mentions standard SQS queues can deliver messages more than once
Designs processing so side effects are idempotent
Mentions dedupe keys, conditional writes, or processed-message state

scoringHints.description: Explains SQS lifecycle, timeout sizing, DLQ policy, idempotency, and monitoring as one reliability design.
Understands visibility timeout and DLQ but misses idempotency or timeout extension.
Knows queues decouple systems but misses core message lifecycle.
Answer risks message loss or uncontrolled duplicate side effects.

followUps.question: What happens if processing takes longer than the visibility timeout?
Which queue metrics would indicate consumers are falling behind?

en.title: Reliable SQS consumer design
en.displayQuestion: How would you build an SQS consumer that processes jobs reliably without losing messages or doing unsafe duplicate work?

guidance: Ask when the message is deleted.
Probe long-running job behavior and poison messages.
commonMistakes: Deleting before successful processing.
Assuming no duplicate delivery.

vi.title: Thiết kế SQS consumer đáng tin cậy

guidance: Hỏi khi nào message được delete.
Đào sâu job chạy lâu và poison message.
commonMistakes: Delete message trước khi xử lý thành công.
Giả định không có duplicate delivery.

ja.title: 信頼性のあるSQS consumer設計

intent: Assess infrastructure-as-code troubleshooting for CloudFormation drift, change sets, and unmanaged console changes.

Inspects the planned update before execution
Identifies replacement actions and production risk before applying changes

Considers updating the template or reverting the manual change
Plans a controlled replacement when replacement is unavoidable

expectedSignals.label: Coordinates backup, maintenance window, rollback plan, and ownership of manual changes.

Addresses ownership and governance risk of manual console changes

requirements.description: Mentions encryption with KMS
Mentions auditing access and versioning or labels where relevant
Explains why secrets should not be copied into source code, Docker images, or earlier image layers

scoringHints.description: Separates config from secrets, chooses the right AWS store, scopes IAM access, and addresses rotation and encryption.
Uses managed stores but misses rotation or fine-grained access details.
Avoids source code secrets but uses ad hoc storage without lifecycle management.
Answer leaves credentials unmanaged or exposed.

followUps.question: When is Parameter Store enough, and when would you pay for Secrets Manager?
Why is deleting a secret from a later Docker layer not enough if it was copied in an earlier layer?

en.title: Secrets and configuration on AWS
en.displayQuestion: Where would you store database credentials and non-secret configuration for an AWS application, and how would you control access and rotation?
en.displayIntent: Check Secrets Manager, Parameter Store, IAM access, KMS encryption, and rotation reasoning.
guidance: Separate secrets from configuration.
Ask how the running workload retrieves values safely.
commonMistakes: Putting secrets in source code or images.
Using one broad IAM permission for every app secret.

vi.title: Secret và configuration trên AWS
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn lưu database credential và non-secret configuration cho application AWS ở đâu, và kiểm soát access/rotation như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra Secrets Manager, Parameter Store, IAM access, KMS encryption và rotation.
guidance: Tách secret khỏi configuration.
Hỏi workload lúc chạy retrieve value an toàn như thế nào.
commonMistakes: Đưa secret vào source code hoặc image.
Dùng một IAM permission quá rộng cho mọi app secret.

ja.title: AWS上のsecretsとconfiguration
ja.displayQuestion: AWSアプリのdatabase credentialと非secret configurationをどこに保存し、accessとrotationをどう制御しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Secrets Manager、Parameter Store、IAM access、KMS encryption、rotationを確認する。
guidance: secretとconfigurationを分ける。
実行中のworkloadが安全に値を取得する方法を聞く。
commonMistakes: secretをsource codeやimageに入れる。
すべてのapp secretへ広いIAM権限を付ける。

sourceReferences.label: Systems Manager Parameter Store
sourceReferences.note: Used for hierarchical config and secret storage capabilities.

sourceReferences.label: Parameter Store and Secrets Manager integration
sourceReferences.note: Used for Secrets Manager reference behavior.

sourceReferences.label: AWS KMS key rotation
sourceReferences.note: Used for KMS rotation behavior.

intent: Assess senior-level judgment around AWS account boundaries, permission guardrails, blast radius, and operational governance.
primaryQuestion: When would you split workloads across multiple AWS accounts, and what guardrails would you put in place?

expectedSignals.label: Uses accounts as isolation boundaries for environments, teams, compliance scopes, and blast-radius reduction.

requirements.description: Uses accounts to isolate environments or teams
Mentions compliance or regulatory scope separation
Connects account boundaries to blast-radius reduction

expectedSignals.label: Mentions centralized identity/federation, temporary credentials, least privilege, and MFA.

requirements.description: Mentions centralized identity or federation
Mentions temporary credentials rather than long-lived users
Mentions least privilege and MFA

expectedSignals.label: Discusses guardrails such as service control policies, permission boundaries, logging/audit aggregation, and network boundaries.

requirements.description: Mentions service control policies
Mentions permission boundaries
Mentions centralized CloudTrail/security findings or network boundaries

expectedSignals.label: Considers operational overhead, shared services, deployment pipelines, cost allocation, and incident response.

requirements.description: Considers operational overhead of multi-account setups
Considers shared services and deployment pipeline complexity
Considers cost allocation and incident response coordination

scoringHints.description: Balances isolation, governance, operations, and developer velocity with concrete account and guardrail patterns.
Understands account isolation but misses one governance dimension such as logging or cost allocation.
Knows multiple accounts are common but cannot explain why or how to operate them.
Answer is either one-account simplicity or uncontrolled account sprawl.

followUps.question: What operational cost does a multi-account setup introduce?
Where would you centralize CloudTrail and security findings?

en.title: AWS multi-account boundaries
en.displayQuestion: When would you split workloads across multiple AWS accounts, and what guardrails would you put in place?
en.displayIntent: Check senior judgment on account isolation, IAM, audit, guardrails, and operating cost.
guidance: Ask for account boundaries and ownership model.
Listen for guardrails plus developer workflow impact.
commonMistakes: Using one account for every environment without controls.
Creating accounts without centralized identity and audit.

vi.title: Ranh giới multi-account AWS
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn tách workload ra nhiều AWS account, và bạn đặt guardrail nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra judgment senior về account isolation, IAM, audit, guardrail và chi phí vận hành.
guidance: Hỏi boundary giữa các account và ownership model.
Nghe guardrail cùng tác động tới workflow developer.
commonMistakes: Dùng một account cho mọi environment mà không có control.
Tạo nhiều account nhưng không centralize identity và audit.

ja.title: AWS multi-account境界
ja.displayQuestion: どのような場合にworkloadを複数AWS accountへ分け、どのguardrailを置きますか。
ja.displayIntent: account isolation、IAM、audit、guardrail、運用コストに関するsenior判断を確認する。
guidance: account境界とownership modelを聞く。
guardrailとdeveloper workflowへの影響を聞き取る。
commonMistakes: すべてのenvironmentをcontrolなしで1 accountに置く。
centralized identityとauditなしでaccountを増やす。

sourceReferences.label: AWS IAM best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for federation, temporary credentials, least privilege, and guardrails.

sourceReferences.label: CloudTrail concepts
sourceReferences.note: Used for audit logging and organization-level governance context.

intent: Check when and how the candidate uses Step Functions for orchestration, retries, catches, timeouts, and stateful workflows.
primaryQuestion: When would you introduce Step Functions instead of chaining Lambda functions directly, and how would you handle task failures?

expectedSignals.label: Uses Step Functions when workflow state, branching, retries, human-visible execution history, or long orchestration matters.

requirements.description: Uses Step Functions when workflow state or branching matters
Mentions managed retries or human-visible execution history
Mentions long orchestration as a Step Functions fit

expectedSignals.label: Avoids tightly chaining Lambdas through direct invokes for complex workflows.

requirements.description: Avoids tightly chaining Lambdas through direct invokes for complex workflows
Explains direct chains can hide coupling and failure paths
Connects Step Functions to clearer orchestration visibility

expectedSignals.label: Uses Retry for transient failures with backoff and Catch for fallback or compensation paths.

requirements.description: Uses Retry for transient failures with backoff
Uses Catch for fallback paths
Mentions compensation paths for failures that should not be retried blindly

expectedSignals.label: Considers timeouts, idempotency, payload size, cost per state transition, and observability.

requirements.description: Considers timeouts and idempotency
Considers payload size and cost per state transition
Monitors stuck workflows, fallback frequency, and failed executions

scoringHints.description: Explains orchestration value and designs retry/catch behavior with clear failure semantics.
Knows Step Functions orchestration and basic Retry/Catch but misses cost or idempotency implications.
Uses Step Functions as a buzzword without workflow error-handling detail.
Answer cannot distinguish orchestration from simple function invocation.

followUps.question: What kinds of errors should not be retried automatically?
How would you detect that a workflow is stuck or frequently entering a fallback path?

en.title: Step Functions orchestration failures
en.displayQuestion: When would you introduce Step Functions instead of chaining Lambda functions directly, and how would you handle task failures?
en.displayIntent: Check orchestration trade-offs, Retry/Catch, timeouts, idempotency, and workflow visibility.
guidance: Ask for a concrete multi-step workflow.
Push on retryable versus non-retryable errors.
commonMistakes: Directly chaining many Lambdas with poor visibility.
Retrying every error with no fallback.

vi.title: Orchestration failure với Step Functions
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn đưa Step Functions vào thay vì chain Lambda trực tiếp, và bạn xử lý task failure như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra trade-off orchestration, Retry/Catch, timeout, idempotency và workflow visibility.
guidance: Yêu cầu một workflow nhiều bước cụ thể.
Đào sâu lỗi nào retry được và lỗi nào không.
commonMistakes: Chain nhiều Lambda trực tiếp với visibility kém.
Retry mọi lỗi mà không có fallback.

ja.title: Step Functionsのorchestration failure
ja.displayQuestion: Lambdaを直接chainする代わりにStep Functionsを導入するのはいつで、task failureをどう扱いますか。
ja.displayIntent: orchestrationのtrade-off、Retry/Catch、timeout、idempotency、workflow visibilityを確認する。
guidance: 具体的な複数step workflowを求める。
retry可能なerrorと不可能なerrorを深掘りする。
commonMistakes: 多くのLambdaを直接chainしてvisibilityが低い。
fallbackなしですべてのerrorをretryする。

sourceReferences.label: Step Functions error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for Retry, Catch, timeout, and error-name behavior.

intent: Evaluate senior incident response behavior for AWS production issues, including triage, mitigation, evidence, and follow-up.
primaryQuestion: During a production incident on AWS, error rates spike after a deployment and someone also changed IAM permissions manually. How do you lead the first 30 minutes?

expectedSignals.label: Stabilizes customer impact first with rollback, traffic shifting, scaling, or feature disablement based on evidence.

requirements.description: Prioritizes stabilizing customer impact first
Uses rollback, traffic shifting, scaling, or feature disablement as mitigation options
Chooses mitigation based on evidence and customer-impact metrics

expectedSignals.label: Separates deployment regression investigation from IAM/audit investigation while coordinating both workstreams.

requirements.description: Separates deployment regression investigation
Separates IAM/audit investigation
Coordinates both workstreams without blocking mitigation

expectedSignals.label: Uses CloudWatch dashboards/logs/alarms and CloudTrail events to build a timeline.

requirements.description: Uses CloudWatch dashboards, logs, and alarms
Uses CloudTrail events for manual IAM permission changes
Builds a timeline from deployment, metrics, logs, and audit events

expectedSignals.label: Communicates status, owner, impact, mitigation, and next checkpoint clearly.

requirements.description: Communicates current status and customer impact
Communicates owners and mitigation actions
Sets the next checkpoint or update cadence

scoringHints.description: Leads with mitigation, clear roles, evidence timeline, audit checks, communication cadence, and post-incident corrective actions.
Handles rollback and basic investigation but misses parallel IAM/audit handling or communication detail.
Focuses on debugging details but lacks incident leadership structure.
Answer is chaotic, blame-oriented, or lacks user-impact mitigation.

followUps.question: What exact customer-impact metric tells you the mitigation worked?
How would you prevent untracked manual IAM changes after the incident?

en.title: AWS production incident leadership
en.displayQuestion: During a production incident on AWS, error rates spike after a deployment and someone also changed IAM permissions manually. How do you lead the first 30 minutes?
en.displayIntent: Check incident leadership, mitigation, AWS evidence gathering, audit trail use, and communication.
guidance: Look for mitigation before root-cause perfection.
Ask how the timeline is built from CloudWatch and CloudTrail.
commonMistakes: Debugging endlessly before reducing impact.
Allowing uncontrolled manual console changes during incident response.

vi.title: Dẫn dắt incident production trên AWS
vi.displayQuestion: Trong một production incident trên AWS, error rate tăng mạnh sau deploy và cũng có người vừa đổi IAM permission thủ công. Bạn dẫn dắt 30 phút đầu như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra leadership khi incident, mitigation, thu thập evidence AWS, dùng audit trail và communication.
guidance: Tìm mitigation trước khi đòi root cause hoàn hảo.
Hỏi cách dựng timeline từ CloudWatch và CloudTrail.
commonMistakes: Debug quá lâu trước khi giảm impact.
Cho phép console change thủ công không kiểm soát trong incident.

ja.title: AWS production incidentのリード
ja.displayQuestion: AWSのproduction incidentで、deployment後にerror rateが急増し、同時に誰かがIAM permissionを手動変更しました。最初の30分をどうリードしますか。
ja.displayIntent: incident leadership、mitigation、AWS evidence収集、audit trail、communicationを確認する。
guidance: 完全なroot causeより先にmitigationを見る。
CloudWatchとCloudTrailからtimelineを作る方法を聞く。
commonMistakes: impactを下げる前に延々とdebugする。
incident中に無制御なconsole変更を許す。

sourceReferences.label: CloudWatch Logs concepts
sourceReferences.note: Used for centralized logs and operational investigation.

sourceReferences.label: CloudTrail event history
sourceReferences.note: Used for management event investigation and timeline reconstruction.

sourceReferences.label: AWS IAM security best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for post-incident credential and permission hygiene.

intent: Assess architecture review maturity using AWS Well-Architected thinking across reliability, security, performance, cost, and operations.
primaryQuestion: You inherit an AWS workload that is reliable but significantly over budget. How would you reduce cost without weakening security or reliability?

expectedSignals.label: Starts with measurement: cost allocation, usage patterns, idle resources, rightsizing, storage lifecycle, data transfer, and reserved or savings options where appropriate.

requirements.description: Starts with cost allocation and usage pattern measurement
Measures idle resources, rightsizing opportunities, storage lifecycle, and data transfer
Considers reserved instances or Savings Plans where appropriate

expectedSignals.label: Protects non-negotiables such as security controls, backups, disaster recovery requirements, and SLOs.

requirements.description: Protects required security controls
Protects backups and disaster recovery requirements
Protects service level objectives or reliability commitments

expectedSignals.label: Uses Well-Architected trade-off thinking across cost optimization, reliability, performance efficiency, and operational excellence.

requirements.description: Balances cost optimization with reliability
Considers performance efficiency
Considers operational excellence and maintainability

expectedSignals.label: Plans safe experiments, rollback criteria, and before/after metrics instead of one-time cuts.

requirements.description: Plans safe experiments rather than one-time cuts
Defines rollback criteria for cost changes
Defines before/after metrics to verify savings and reliability impact

scoringHints.description: Builds a measured cost-reduction plan that preserves SLOs and security while tracking before/after impact.
Identifies common cost levers but misses risk controls or success metrics.
Suggests generic cost cutting without workload-specific evidence.
Answer would likely harm reliability or security.

followUps.question: Which three metrics would you put on the cost optimization plan?
Which cost-saving action would you refuse unless the business accepted a reliability trade-off?

en.title: Well-Architected cost optimization
en.displayQuestion: You inherit an AWS workload that is reliable but significantly over budget. How would you reduce cost without weakening security or reliability?
en.displayIntent: Check Well-Architected cost optimization with security, reliability, operations, and measurement trade-offs.
guidance: Ask for before/after metrics.
Look for explicit protection of SLOs, backups, and security controls.
commonMistakes: Cutting redundancy before understanding risk.
Only rightsizing EC2 while ignoring storage and data transfer.

vi.title: Cost optimization theo Well-Architected
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn tiếp quản một AWS workload khá reliable nhưng vượt ngân sách nhiều. Bạn giảm cost như thế nào mà không làm yếu security hoặc reliability?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cost optimization theo Well-Architected cùng trade-off về security, reliability, operations và measurement.
guidance: Hỏi metric trước/sau.
Tìm cam kết bảo vệ SLO, backup và security control.
commonMistakes: Cắt redundancy trước khi hiểu risk.
Chỉ rightsize EC2 mà bỏ qua storage và data transfer.

ja.title: Well-Architectedなcost optimization
ja.displayQuestion: 信頼性はあるが大きく予算超過しているAWS workloadを引き継ぎました。securityやreliabilityを弱めずにcostをどう下げますか。
ja.displayIntent: Well-Architectedに基づくcost optimizationと、security、reliability、operations、measurementのtrade-offを確認する。
guidance: before/after metricsを聞く。
SLO、backup、security controlを明示的に守るかを見る。
commonMistakes: riskを理解する前に冗長性を削る。
EC2のrightsizeだけ見てstorageとdata transferを無視する。

sourceReferences.label: AWS Well-Architected Framework pillars
sourceReferences.note: Used for cost, reliability, security, performance, and operational trade-off framing.

sourceReferences.label: AWS Well-Architected Tool overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for review and improvement-plan framing.
